frozen_components: target length predictor (linear regression, fitted on validation set)
architecture.summary: Control-DAG is a constrained decoding algorithm for Directed Acyclic T5 (DA-T5), a non-autoregressive model that generates Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) representing output hypotheses. The system converts DAGs to Weighted Finite State Automata (WFSAs), then intersects them with constraint FSAs encoding lexical requirements, valid vocabulary, and target length constraints. A pruned Depth-First Search Viterbi algorithm searches the constrained WFSA to find the highest-scoring output satisfying all constraints. Information flows from the input through DA-T5 (producing a DAG), through DAG-to-WFSA conversion and pruning, through FSA intersection operations, and finally through DFS-Viterbi path search to yield constrained output text.

biblio.title: Control-DAG: Constrained Decoding for Non-Autoregressive Directed Acyclic T5 using Weighted Finite State Automata

claims.metric: Slot Error Rate (SER) and Neologism Rate (NEO)
claims.statement: Control-DAG achieves zero slot error rate (SER=0.0) and zero neologism rate (NEO=0.0) on the Schema Guided Dialogue dataset

baseline.name: auto-regressive T5-small with constrained beam search

claims.statement: Control-DAG on DA-T5-small achieves BLEURT 60.0 and BLEU 22.9 on SGD
Control-DAG achieves 1.7x speedup relative to auto-regressive constrained beam search on SGD
Control-DAG on DA-T5-small achieves BLEURT 46.8 and BLEU 19.0 on DART with zero exact occurrence error rate (EOR=0.0) and zero neologism rate (NEO=0.0)
Control-DAG achieves 1.4x speedup relative to auto-regressive constrained beam search on DART

claims.dataset: Schema Guided Dialogue (SGD) and Data-to-Text tasks
claims.statement: DA-T5 without Control-DAG exhibits frequent out-of-vocabulary (OOV) errors (>20% neologism rate) and fails to generate specified entity names (up to 40% slot error rate)
Hard Lexical Constraints eliminate slot errors by constructing FSAs that enforce designated phrases must appear in the output

The paper leaves further training and model scaling to future work due to focus on decoding algorithms; further inference speed improvements are possible through C implementation of DAG-to-WFSA conversion and DFS-Viterbi but are not explored
